REQUEST_CHANGES
**service_adr005_test.go:230-247 — `TestResolveRouteThreadsAutoSelectionInputs` is misleading.** The test name and AC#6 imply a round-trip check that `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens`/`RequiresTools` reach the engine and apply context-window/tool gates. artifact: .ddx/executions/20260425T084734-a9fd23bd/reviewer-stream.log

post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**service_adr005_test.go:230-247 — `TestResolveRouteThreadsAutoSelectionInputs` is misleading.** The test name and AC#6 imply a round-trip check that `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens`/`RequiresTools` reach the engine and apply context-window/tool gates. The body never calls `ResolveRoute`, builds an unused `svc`, and only asserts `routing.Request.MinContextWindow() > 0` — which is a property of the internal `routing` package, not of the public surface threading. Either:
(a) Replace with a real test that constructs a service with a fake catalog + provider, calls `svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, RouteRequest{EstimatedPromptTokens: 1_000_000, RequiresTools: true})`, and asserts the returned candidate carries `FilterReason == FilterReasonContextTooSmall` (or `FilterReasonNoToolSupport`), proving the field survived the round trip; or
(b) Rename it to `TestRouteRequestMinContextWindowDerivedFromEstimatedTokens` and drop the dead `svc` to make scope honest.
**Minor — `deriveFilterReason` keyword classifier is brittle.** `service_routing.go:120-141` matches on substrings of free-form `Reason` text emitted by the internal engine; a future engine wording tweak (e.g. "ctx window") silently demotes the rejection to `FilterReasonScoredBelowTop`. Not blocking for step 1, but worth filing a follow-up to surface the filter reason as a typed field on `routing.Candidate` rather than re-parsing a string.
